briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome. We will now begin the climate briefing for June 23, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Record-Breaking Heatwaves: A Global Emergency
sections.content: Europe is currently facing a severe crisis due to record-breaking heatwaves. Human casualties are rapidly increasing, with 40 people drowning in France in less than ten days and temperatures in the UK projected to reach 39℃ [11, 75]. This extreme heat has even led to the paradoxical situation where a climate change event discussing heatwave adaptation had to be canceled [42]. Extreme heatwave phenomena are also increasing in various regions worldwide, including Western Massachusetts [44], and there are warnings that while the ocean has protected us from the worst of climate change, it is now suffering from a 'fever' [55]. These catastrophic heatwaves could lead to economic impacts, such as a surge in livestock product prices [38]. Although countries are taking emergency measures to cope with the heatwaves [71, 74], criticism is also being raised that adaptation plans are dangerously delayed amidst escalating risks [77].

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Action Discussions and Challenges
sections.content: The international community recognizes the importance of climate action and continues various discussions, but concerns about policy weakening and the voices of vulnerable populations are growing louder. Countries are engaging in international cooperation for climate action, with the Republic of Korea's Ambassador for Climate Change attending the 10th Ministerial Meeting on Climate Action [1]. The UN Secretary-General is demonstrating a proactive stance, presenting a blueprint for clean energy transition solutions to address the climate crisis and demanding that AI companies disclose their environmental costs [12, 45, 56]. However, the UK's chief climate advisor warned that weakening net-zero policies would harm the economy [41, 72], and there are strong voices asserting that ignoring climate change poses risks to ourselves [53]. In particular, climate change has been linked to stunted growth in African children [60], and developing countries like Bangladesh are urging for more support to address climate change [65]. These voices, most affected by the environmental crisis, are pointing to climate change as a 'form of oppression' [40, 79].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Change Response and Carbon Neutrality Implementation Efforts
sections.content: Domestically, society-wide efforts to address climate change are actively underway, and concrete measures for carbon neutrality implementation are being explored. Collaboration between public institutions and local communities is strengthening, with aT and the National Institute of Agricultural Sciences signing an MOU for climate change response cooperation [2, 13], and the Daejeon-Sejong-Chungnam Climate Council holding a joint forum [8]. Public institutions, universities, and international organizations have gathered to enhance water management and climate change response capabilities in developing countries [9]. The necessity of investment in climate change adaptation is also being emphasized, such as releasing young flatfish to restore fishery resources diminished by climate change [10, 3]. Furthermore, citizen-participatory carbon neutrality initiatives are expanding, exemplified by Anseong City's establishment of a 'Climate Action Opportunity Income' platform [16, 22] and Seongnam City's recruitment of participants for its 'Carbon Neutral Living Lab' [18, 20]. The Korea Environment Corporation is also preparing for the future by training greenhouse gas specialists [23]. Additionally, experts from the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) are discussing the impact of climate change on nuclear power generation [5], and a conference exploring climate crisis solutions through AI is scheduled to be held [6].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Demands for Power Market Improvement
sections.content: The acceleration of the transition to renewable energy is a positive sign, but concerns are being raised that the current power market structure could hinder this trend. Private sector investment is active, with the cumulative investment in the renewable energy investment platform 'Mohae' surpassing 500 billion won [26, 29, 37], and Coway expanding its solar power plant at the Yugu factory to increase renewable energy [14, 36]. Furthermore, domestic companies are prominent in the renewable energy sector, with GS E&C expanding new and renewable energy development projects with global investors [32], and the Korea Energy Agency promoting K-renewable energy technology at 'Intersolar Europe' [33]. However, despite the government's renewable energy expansion policies, there is criticism that the power market remains unchanged [27], and the industry's voice is growing louder, demanding improvements in output control that impede renewable energy expansion and revisions to power operation rules [30, 34]. Regarding the correlation between renewable energy expansion and increased power outages, a claim made by some, the prevailing counter-argument is that it is 'not true' [28, 31, 39].

sections.title: 📰 Importance of Climate Science Research and Public Awareness Enhancement
sections.content: Scientific research and efforts to raise public awareness are continuously underway to understand and respond to the complexities of climate change. Globally most-cited climate scientists, such as Professor Detlef van Vuuren and Professor Philip Cia, are contributing to climate change research [52, 82, 83]. Research is also being conducted on how various creatures, including snakes, lizards, and whale sharks [50, 63], cope with climate change. The insurance industry is emphasizing the importance of risk management by establishing a 'common language' for the hidden risks of climate change [43], and research on how the weather of 1903 would appear today under climate change sheds light on long-term changes [58]. Alongside these, activities aimed at increasing public awareness of climate change and encouraging action, such as student podcast projects [49] and tree-planting initiatives for climate change mitigation [51], are also gaining attention.

briefing.closing: As discussed in today's briefing, the world is recognizing the severity of record-breaking heatwaves and climate change, and is making efforts to respond. International cooperation, domestic carbon neutrality practices, and the acceleration of renewable energy transition will play crucial roles in overcoming the current crisis. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: On the 23rd, a citizen looks at a display at a Seoul supermarket as chicken prices soar due to avian influenza and other factors. Reporter Han Su-bin. As prices for livestock products such as eggs and chicken continue to rise, household food costs are also facing an emergency. This is due to supply shortages caused by avian influenza (AI) and higher import prices for pork and other products due to high exchange rates. The government is taking measures such as importing alternative products, but with the heatwave...

summary.phenomenon: On the 23rd, a citizen looks at a display at a Seoul supermarket as chicken prices soar due to avian influenza and other factors. Reporter Han Su-bin. As prices for livestock products such as eggs and chicken continue to rise, household food costs are also facing an emergency, due to supply shortages caused by avian influenza (AI) and high exchange rates for pork.

articles.original_description: 조류인플루엔자 등의 영향으로 닭고기 가격이 고공행진 중인 23일 서울 한 마트에서 시민이 매대를 보고 있다. 한수빈 기자계란과 닭고기 등 축산물 가격이 고공행진을 이어가면서 밥상물가에도 비상이 걸렸다. 조류인플루엔자(AI) 영향으로 인한 공급 부족과 고환율로 돼지고기 등의 수입가격이 비싸진 영향이다.정부는 대체품을 수입하는 등 조치를 취하고 있으나 폭염으로···

articles.title: Do Power Outages Increase with More Renewable Energy? 'No Correlation'
articles.original_title: 재생에너지 늘면 정전도 는다?···“상관관계 없다”
articles.description: High-voltage transmission towers stand in Pyeongtaek, Gyeonggi Province. Senior Reporter Seo Seong-il. Contrary to the common belief that an increase in renewable energy generation leads to more power outages, an analysis has found no consistent correlation between the two variables. It was also determined that major blackouts worldwide recently were often caused by power infrastructure issues such as aging equipment and transmission line failures, rather than the expansion of renewable energy. Renewable energy fact-checking platform...

summary.phenomenon: High-voltage transmission towers stand in Pyeongtaek, Gyeonggi Province. Senior Reporter Seo Seong-il. Contrary to the common belief that an increase in renewable energy generation leads to more power outages, an analysis has found no consistent correlation between the two variables. It was also determined that major blackouts worldwide recently were often caused by power infrastructure issues such as aging equipment and transmission line failures, rather than the expansion of renewable energy.

articles.original_description: 경기 평택 일대에 서 있는 고압 송전탑. 서성일 선임기자재생에너지 발전 비중이 늘어나면 정전도 증가한다는 통념과 달리, 두 변수 사이에 일관된 상관관계는 없다는 분석이 나왔다. 최근 전 세계에서 발생한 주요 대정전 역시 재생에너지 확대보다는 노후 장비와 송전선로 고장 등 전력 인프라 문제에서 비롯된 경우가 많았던 것으로 파악됐다.재생에너지 팩트체크 플랫폼 ···

articles.original_title: A super El Niño threatens disaster. Trump is handling it recklessly | Terry Garcia
articles.description: The administration interrupted data streams that are key to forecasting. These systems should not be vulnerable to political whimsIn 1877, North Americans experienced an unusually mild winter – it was known as the “year without a winter”. It coincided with one of the strongest El Niño events ever recorded. Scientists suspect the same El Niño was a major factor in one of the worst environmental disasters in history. As much of the world was enveloped in drought, harvests collapsed in India, China
